author: m.kalinin
content: do i understand correctly that builders are supposed to deposit via deposit contract prior fork with `0x03` creds? and once builders are onboarded at the fork boundary, `0x03` in the validator withdrawal creds prefix won't denote a builder instance ever?

author: nflaig
content: yes

author: barnabasbusa
content: https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-6.ethpandaops.io/builder/0?v=blocks

author: barnabasbusa
content: take a look at this builder

author: barnabasbusa
content: it was deposited on epoch 5 , we had a pending deposit queue of validator top ups

author: barnabasbusa
content: and gloas went live on epoch 30

author: barnabasbusa
content: overview of the builder deposits: https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-6.ethpandaops.io/builders/deposits

author: m.kalinin
content: what if we use `0x80` instead of `0x03`? `0x03` can become a validator withdrawal creds maybe even soon. Builder withdrawal creds prefix could even start from `0x80`, although, not sure if it could be useful anyhow

author: barnabasbusa
content: this can only be done post the fork

author: barnabasbusa
content: as pre fork the new sys contract is non interactable

author: barnabasbusa
content: and 0x00 is of course a validator type already in the validator system contract

author: m.kalinin
content: There are no constraints on depositing with whatever creds prefix in the validator contract. I just don't like using `0x03` much as it can become a withdrawal creds prefix of the next validator type if we introduce it

author: barnabasbusa
content: 0x00 is already taken tho for BLS key validators no?

author: barnabasbusa
content: how would you differentiate a validator from a builder deposit pre fork?

author: barnabasbusa
content: lets say 1 d before the fork a random dude deposits a 0x00 validator with 32eth

author: m.kalinin
content: i am suggesting something large, like `0x80` that will unlikely become a validator creds prefix in a foreseeable future

author: nflaig
content: I don't think it matters, we wanna deprecate `0x03` after the fork and free it up for validators to use, but technically we could use any prefix

author: m.kalinin
content: it can be `0xf7`, `0x80` could be good if we want to distinguish validator creds and builder creds space

author: barnabasbusa
content: oh my bad

author: barnabasbusa
content: that 8 looked like a 0

author: barnabasbusa
content: need to get my eyes checked

author: m.kalinin
content: suppose we have to introduce `0x03` for builders in the future, could deprecation and then re-introduction cause potential glitches in tooling?

author: barnabasbusa
content: 0xb for builder 😄

author: m.kalinin
content: it's actully nice

author: nflaig
content: <@592004585506340885>'s argument was that `0x03` was established already, but I generally agree, it seems more error pronse to use it, I think there is still time to use something else

author: tersec
content: post-Gloas, builders don't come in the same contract

author: nflaig
content: but he means later, like I* or so, when there is a v4 builder

author: nflaig
content:
https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5359#discussion_r3442172938
> A postteriori it would've been cooler to have this one be 0x0B or 0xBB

author: nflaig
content: I support `0x0B` reads like "version 0 builder"

author: m.kalinin
content: i think `0xB0` is better just because it is much further away from `0x02` 😄

author: m.kalinin
content: sure, i am trying to address potential future clash between `0x03` builder pre-Gloas and `0x03` validator after Gloas. Even though the probability of such clash is quite minimal, it seems like it's completely free for us to avoid this potential problem

author: tersec
content: sure why not, I don't disagree
